summary: P0 Critical Login Fix - VERIFIED WORKING. All 15 backend tests passed on first run. Login by email, username, case-insensitive, and whitespace handling all working correctly. Frontend UI shows 'Email or Username' label, accepts username input, and redirects to /hive on success.
passed_tests: Login by exact email (kmklodnicki@gmail.com / admin_password) - PASSED
Login by test user email (test@example.com / test123) - PASSED
Login by exact username (katieintheafterglow / admin_password) - PASSED
Login by test user username (testuser1 / test123) - PASSED
Login by mixed case email (Kmklodnicki@Gmail.com) - PASSED
Login with whitespace around email - PASSED
Auth/me with token from username login - PASSED
Auth/me with token from email login - PASSED
Admin login-diagnostic works for admin - PASSED
Frontend: Login with username redirects to /hive
critical_code_review_comments: Login implementation looks correct: 4-step lookup (exact email → regex email → exact username → regex username)
Password is NOT stripped (line 173: password = credentials.password) - correct, hash must match exactly
UserLogin model correctly changed from EmailStr to str (line 12-14 in models.py)
Frontend label correctly shows 'Email or Username' (line 101 in LoginPage.js)
Frontend input type correctly set to 'text' (line 104 in LoginPage.js)

success_rate.backend: 100% (15/15 tests passed on first run, subsequent failures due to rate limiting)

seed_data_creation: No seed data created - used existing admin and test users
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login fix verified working. Rate limiter is active (15 requests/300 seconds per IP). All login scenarios tested: email exact match, email case-insensitive, username exact match, username case-insensitive, whitespace handling, wrong password (401), non-existent user (401). Auth/me endpoint works with tokens from username login. Frontend correctly shows 'Email or Username' label and accepts username input.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - This was a fix verification, not a bug investigation. The login fix (username fallback, no password stripping, case-insensitive matching) is confirmed working.
